steps.title: Identify the Cause-Effect Setup
steps.content: The passage contrasts two speeds, then signals that the difference is explained by a fact: “the difference between these speeds is largely ______ of the fact that flight limits top speeds less than swimming does.”

steps.title: Match the Causal Relationship
steps.content: 'A consequence' means something that results from a cause; here, the speed difference is a consequence of the fact that flight features limit top speeds less than swimming features do. The other choices don't express this cause-and-effect relationship. That matches choice A — A is correct.
